name: Meatloaf
directions: Line loaf pan with bacon.
Mix all the rest of the ingredients together.
Place mixture into bacon-lined loaf pan.
Bake for 1 1/2 to 1 3/4 hours in a 350 degree oven.
Baste the meatloaf occasionally with the drippings.

name: Tasty Cheeseburger Meatloaf
directions: In a bowl, combine 1/4 cup ketchup, egg, bread crumbs and onion powder.
Crumble ground beef over mixture and mix well.
On a large sheet of wax paper, pat beef mixture into a 10x6-inch rectangle.
Spread remaining ketchup over meat to within 1/2 inch of long sides and 1-1/2 inches on short sides.
Top with mustard and relish.
Place 4 slices of cheese on top, set remaining cheese aside.
Roll up loaf, jelly roll style, starting with short side and pulling away wax paper while rolling.
Seal seams and ends well.
Place loaf seam side down on a greased 11x7x2-inch baking pan.
Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes or until meat is no longer pink and a meat thermometer reads 160°F.
cut the reserved cheese slices diagonally; place on top of loaf.
Return to oven for 5 minutes or until cheese is melted.
Let stand for 10 minutes before slicing.

name: Barbecue Beef
directions: Preheat oven 350°F.
Place beef/pork roast in a baking dish (I use a bean pot).
Combine all ingredients pour over meat bake for 3 hours.

name: Hamburger Patties
directions: Sometimes I vary the seasonings- using a steak seasoning or Cajun seasoning instead of salt and pepper.
Mix ingredients with your hands using a light touch.
Don't be tempted to overmix.
If you do, it becomes too dense.
When charcoal grilling, add 1/2 cup water to the beef mixture to keep it from drying out.

For pan frying, don't use a nonstick pan- it won't brown as well, and heat some oil in the pan before adding burgers to keep the burgers from sticking and to help sear and form a nice brown, crispy exterior.
Either method will take about 10- 12 minutes of cooking time, depending on thickness of patty, temperature of heat source and your preference (we like ours medium cooked).
Note: when pan-frying don't be tempted to press down and squeeze all the juice out, just turn them more often (turn only once on the grill).

name: Easy Weeknight Meatballs
directions: In a large bowl beat the egg.
Add the remaining ingredients and blend together with your hands.
Roll the mixture into 1 1/2" balls with your hands and place on non-stick cookie sheet so that they are not touching.
Cook in pre-heated oven for 10 minutes at 450 degrees.
Turn meatballs over half way through cooking.
The meatballs will not be fully cooked at this point.
Add to your favorite sauce and simmer for 1 hour.
Serve over pasta.

name: Diane's Meatballs
directions: Mix all ingredients except flour and paprika.
Shape into 1 1/2"- 2" balls.
Heat oil in skillet and add meatballs.
Brown meatballs on all sides, remove from pan and set aside.
Add flour to drippings and brown to your liking, add enough water to make gravy of desired consistency.
Add meatballs to gravy and simmer until done.
Sprinkle with paprika.

name: Basic Meatloaf
directions: Preheat oven to 350.
Mix all ingredients in a bowl.
Spray a loaf pan with a non-stick veg spray (Pam).
Put meat mixture in the loaf pan.
Cover with foil.
Bake for about 1 1/2 hours.

name: Steak Burgers
directions: Mix all ingredients together and form into oblong rolls.
Broil or barbecue until desired doneness.

name: Meatloaf
directions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Mix ketchup, brown sugar and vinegar in a small bowl (this will be used to top meat loaf as a glaze) Heat oil in a skillet adding onion and garlic; sauté until tender.
Mix eggs with salt, pepper, thyme, mustard, Worcestershire and milk; add to ground beef and pork in a large bowl along with crackers, parsley and cooked onion/garlic mixture.

Blend meat mixture until thoroughly mixed (if meat mixture sticks to side of the bowl add a slight amount more milk) Place mixture in a foil lined baking pan and shape into a loaf (approx 9x5 inches) Brush top with glaze mixture.
Bake for approx 1 hour (check for doneness) Let cool for about 15 minutes before serving.
NOTE: Try frying a couple of slices of the cold leftovers in a small amount of butter for a great hot sandwich!

name: Blue Cheese Meatloaf
directions: Combine 1 cup bread crumbs and 1/4 cup crumbled blue cheese.
Add ground beef, egg, onion, salt and pepper.
Blend thoroughly.
Shape into a round loaf.
With a spoon, make a crater-like depression in top of loaf.
Fill depression with Blue Cheese Dressing.
If desired, sprinkle more crumbled blue cheese on top.
Bake at 350 for 1 hour.

name: Italian Meatballs
directions: Combine all ingredients well in a large bowl.
Shape into equal sized balls IQF on cookie sheets, when frozen place in large freezer bags.
To cook, either frozen or fresh, place in gently simmering spaghetti sauce and simmer until cooked through and tender.

name: Onion Burgers
directions: Combine beef, soup mix and worcestershire sauce, mixing well with your hands.
Divide meat mixture into 4 equal parts and form into patties.
Cook on grill or in a pan on the stovetop.
Before removing the patties from heat, place the cheese slices on top, so they have a second to melt.
Serve with rolls and all your favorite toppings!

name: My Favorite Meatloaf
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Form into a loaf and place in a foil-lined bread pan. (There usually is about 2 inches of space on both ends between the meatloaf and the pan).
Making sure the top is flat to insure even cooking.
You can top it with extra ketchup, if desired.
Bake at 425°F for 45 minutes.

name: Buttermilk Meatloaf
directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
Grease a bread pan and line with bread slices.
Soak crumbs in milk for 5 minutes and stir in oregano, thyme, salt, pepper, eggs, and worcestershire.
Add meat, carrot, onion, and mix well.
Shape into loaf, place in pan, and flatten top.
Mix sugar, mustard, and ketchup and pour half over loaf.
Bake 30 minutes, brush with remaining sauce, and bake another 30 minutes.
Remove from oven and let sit 15 or 20 minutes.
Remove from pan, discard bread, slice and serve.

name: Lea & Perrins Bacon and Cheese Burgers
directions: In medium bowl, combine all ingredients; shape into 3 or 4 patties.
Grill or broil until done, these may also be pan-fried!
Top with New Lea & Perrins Table Sauce, for a great finish! (I top mine with ketchup and a little horseradish sauce!).

similar.name: All Mixed up Bacon Cheese Burgers

name: Swedish Meatballs
directions: Soak crumbs in milk.
Saute onion in butter; add to crumbs.
Add seasonings and mix well with egg, then the ground beef.
Blend well.
Form into 1 inch balls.
Roll in flour.
Saute in butter till brown.
Add 1 cup water with bouillon cubes.
Cover, simmer gently 5 minutes.
Remove meat, thicken broth to make gravy.
Pour over meat balls and serve.

name: Bacon and Blue Burgers
directions: Mix the meat with the worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper and form into 8 patties.
Combine the bacon and blue cheese and divide between half the patties.
Place the remaining patties on top and press the edges to seal.
Grill over hot coals, pan fry, or broil until cooked through, turning once halfway through cooking.

similar.name: All Mixed up Bacon Cheese Burgers

name: (T W A) Meatballs for Spaghetti
directions: Mix all ingredients together. Remember, the less you handle the more tender they will be.
Shape into 1-inch size balls.
Bake on a broiler pan for approximately 20 minutes in 375 degree Fahrenheit oven, turning often until done. Meatballs can also be fried in a skillet.
Add to recipe #128830 TWA spaghetti sauce and simmer over low heat approximately 2 hours. Stir often to prevent scorching.

name: Awesome Meatloaf
directions: Mix everything together. Then put 1/2 of hamburger mixture in, then 3-4 cheese slices, then the rest of the hamburger mixture.
Bake for 1 hour. 20 minutes before its finished, drizzle a bit more russian dressing on top.

name: Buffalo Meatballs
directions: Preheat oven to 325 degrees.
Combine thoroughly all ingredients except water.  Add water a little at a time until creamy texture is achieved.
Form meat mixture into 16 equal balls.
Place on broiling pan, spacing evenly.
Place pan on middle rack in oven.
Bake approximately 20 minutes, turning once towards the end. May need a few additional minutes, depending on individual oven.

name: Ranch Cheeseburgers
directions: Heat gas grill.
In medium bowl mix beef, onion, parsley, 3 tablespoons of the dressing and pepper.
Shape mixture into 4 patties about 3/4 inch thick.
Place patties on grill; close. Grill 4 to 6 minutes or until meat thermometer inserted in center of patties reads 160 F and patties are no longer pink in the center.
Top each patty with cheese slice.
Let stand on grill until cheese is melted.
Spread remaining ranch dressing on cut sides of buns. Place burgers on buns.

name: Italian Meatballs
directions: Mix everything and form into balls.
Brown them just a little bit and then put them in your tomato gravy.
Cook on low for a couple of hours.

name: Seasoned Hamburger Mix
directions: In a large skillet, cook beef and onions in two batches over medium heat until meat is no longer pink; drain. In a large bowl, combine the beef mixture, salt, oregano, pepper and garlic salt. Freeze in 2-cup portions for up to 3 months.

name: Basic Meatballs Recipe
directions: In large bowl, beat eggs and stir in bread crumbs until well combined. Stir in milk, onion, parsley, salt and mustard. Add meat and knead with wet hands until well blended. Pat meat into rectangle about 1-inch thick. Cut into 1-inch squares and form each square into ball

Place meat balls in large shallow baking pan and bake in 400: F oven for about 25 minutes or until browned. With slotted spoon, transfer meatballs to trays, spacing balls so they are not touching. Let cool, then freeze until firm. Package in heavy plastic bags, seal securely and store in freezer.

name: Martha Stewart Meatballs
directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Place breadcrumbs in a small bowl. Drizzle the milk over and let stand until absorbed.
Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the onion and garlic; cook until translucent, about 3 minutes. Set aside.

Place the ground meats in a large bowl. Add breadcrumb mixture to meats along with the reserved onion and garlic, whole eggs, yolks, parsley, oregano, and cheese. Season with salt and pepper. Using your hands, mix until just combined. Moisten hands with water, and roll meat mixture into 3-inch meatballs. Place on a baking sheet.

Heat 1/4 cup of oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Working in batches, add meatballs, and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes. Finish cooking in sauce, or transfer to a baking sheet fitted with a rack to drain. Bake until cooked through, 12 to 15 minutes.

name: Meatloaf  (No Ketchup)
directions: preheat oven to 425.
mix all ingredients in a bowl.
make sure to break up all the egg.
place meatloaf mixture on nonstick cookie sheet (one with sides).
form a loaf with your hands.
bake 1 hour at 425.

name: Grilled Garlic and Herb Burgers
directions: Preheat grill for medium-high heat.
In a large mixing bowl, combine meat, herbs, garlic, bread crumbs, egg, lemon juice, salt, and pepper until well mixed.
Split mixture into four equal portions.
Shape into rectangular patties about 5-6 inches long.
Place patties on lightly oiled grill grate and cook for 8-10 minutes on each side to the degree of doneness that you desire.
Remove from heat and serve on buns.

name: Belles Garlic Meatballs
directions: In a mixing bowl, combine the chuck and pork.
Add salt, cayenne, black pepper, Worcestershire sauce, oregano, and the egg. Mix well.
Form the mixture into 12 small balls and insert the garlic into the center of the balls (1/2 a clove per meatball).
Dredge the meatballs in the flour and shake off any excess.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium heat. Brown the meatballs, turning several times, and cook for about 15 minute.
Drain off any excess fat and add the beef broth, cover skillet with a lid and simmer for about 15 minute (If using these for spaghetti then just add them to your sauce now to finish cooking.).

name: Large Batch Baked Meatballs
directions: Combine bread and milk in a small bowl; let soak for 10 minutes.  Squeeze bread to release milk; discard milk.
Combine rest of ingredients, shaping into small balls and placing on roasting tray sprayed with Pam.
Bake in 350 oven for 20 - 30 minutes, depending on size of meatball.  Remove from oven and tilt tray to drain fat.  Let meatballs cool before packaging.
Freeze up to one month.
Note:  I have used whole wheat and flax seed bread with good results.

name: Ultimate Steakhouse Burger
directions: Fry bacon in skillet. Place 3 tablespoons of bacon fat into heatproof bowl and set in refrigerator while preparing other ingredients.
Put bread in bowl, add milk, and let mixture sit about 5 minutes. Using a fork, mash bread and milk till smooth paste forms.

Break up beef into small bits into a medium size bowl. Season with salt and pepper, then add garlic, bread paste, and reserved chilled bacon fat. Lightly knead all ingredients to mix and incorporate. Divide into 4 patties.

Oil cooking grate and grill burgers over a very hot fire, without squeezing down on them, until well seared on both sides (7-10 mins depending on preference for doneness). Top burgers with bacon previously cooked. Serve on buns.

name: Easy Cheesy Topped Burgers
directions: Heat grill to medium.
Mix cheese, bacon, mayonnaise, ketchup and pepper sauce in small bowl.
Grill burgers almost to desired doneness.
Top each burger with 1/4 C of cheese mixture; cook, covered, until cheese is melted, about 3-4 minutes.
Serve on hamburger buns.

similar.name: All Mixed up Bacon Cheese Burgers

name: Cheddar Meatloaf
directions: Combine ground beef, oatmeal,cheese, 1/2 cup barbecue sauce, onion, garlic powder and egg.
Shape in shallow baking dish.
Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes.
Brush with remaining barbecue sauce.
Bake 15 minutes longer.

name: Jerbear Meatloaf
directions: Take 3 slices of bread and tear them in to pieces and add them to the ingredients.
Mix the ingredients in a large bowl using your hands.
Preheat oven to 350°F put mixture into a loaf pan.
Let meatloaf cook for 1 hour 15 minutes.
After cooking, let it sit for 10-15 minutes then prepare as you wish.

name: Great Meatballs (For Anything)
directions: Combine ingredients and shape into balls (I don't make them too big).
At this point you can brown them in a skillet over medium heat till done, OR you can put them on a cookie sheet and bake them in a 350 degree oven till done--this is what I do most of the time.
If you are going to freeze them or a portion of them, arrange them on a baking sheet and when frozen put them in a freeze bag and you will be able to take out what you want for whatever you want.

name: Healthy Meatballs
directions: Mix all ingredients together and form into 1 1/2 inch balls.
Put in top of steamer basket.
Put 1/4 cup water in bottom of steamer. Cover and cook 5 to 8 mins on high or until cooked through.
Rinse under HOT water for about 5 minutes.
Use in normal way you would use meatballs.
To freeze, flash freeze meatballs and then place in a freezer bag. Use as many or as few as needed.
Makes about 20 meatballs 3 per serving.

name: Un-Traditional Cheeseburgers
directions: Mix all ingredients except cheese slices.
Form into 6 patties.
Grill and add cheese in the last 2 minutes of grilling.
Toast buns and serve.

name: Stuffed Meatloaf
directions: In a large bowl, combine the first 10 ingredients. Crumble beef over mixture and mix well.
On a large piece of tin foil, form the beef mixture into a rectangle about 1 inch thick. Spread sour cream all over the top. Sprinkle the cheddar and olives on top.
Roll up, jelly-roll style. Seal seam and ends. Place seam side down in a greased baking dish.
Bake, uncovered, at 350 degrees for about 45 minute or until the meat is no longer pink.

name: Souped up Burgers
directions: Mix all ingredients in a bowl and form into patties.
Grill 6 minutes on each side or to your preference of cooking.  This makes medium rare burgers.

name: Yum Yum Meatloaf
directions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Gently mix all the ingredients together. Too much mixing may result in a tough meatloaf.
Pack into a loaf pan and bake for about an hour.

name: My Italian Meatballs
directions: Heat oven to 350 degrees.

In a large bowl combine all the ingredients with a large wooden spoon or fork or use your hands.(This adds the Love!).  Incorporate it all together gently, being careful not to over mix.  Form into 24 meatballs and place in a 9x13 metal baking pan. Bake for 350 degrees for 45 minutes.

name: Double Cheese Meatloaf
directions: Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
Saute chopped onion for three minutes over medium-high heat.
Combine onion, 4 teaspoons of the ketchup, 1 teaspoon of the mustard, Mozzarella, bread crumbs, parsley, Parmesan, oregano, pepper and the egg in a large bowl.
Crumble ground meat over the mixture and blend well using your hands.
Pack mixture into a loaf pan coated with cooking spray.
Combine remaining ketchup and mustard and spread over the top.
Bake 50 minutes or until a meat thermometer registers 160 degrees.
Let meatloaf stand for 10 minutes before serving.

name: Juicy Italian Meatballs
directions: Combine crumbs, cheese, seasonings, eggs and water, blend well.
Add meat and mix together very well.
Prepare cookie sheet with pan spray.
Roll into balls.
Place on sheet pan and bake.

name: Marvelous Meatloaf
directions: Mix together all ingredients except cheese slices.
Bake at 375% for one hour.
For last 5 minutes, top with the cheese slices to melt.
Let stand for a couple of minutes for easy slicing.

name: Make-Ahead Meatballs
directions: In a large bowl, beat eggs.
Add the next five ingredients.
Add beef; mix well.
Shape into 1-in. balls, about 12 dozen.
Place meatballs on greased racks in shallow baking pans.
Bake at 400 for 10-15 minutes or until no longer pink, turning often; drain.
Cool.
Place about 30 meatballs each into freezer containers.
May be frozen for up to 3 months.
Yield: 5 batches (about 30 meatballs per batch).

name: Meatloaf
directions: Knead all ingredients together.
Cook at 350 degrees for 1 to 1 1/2 hours.

name: Granny's Meatballs
directions: Combine all the ingredients except the olive oil and garlic  in a bowl. Add the olive oil and garlic in a small sauce pan and heat. Roll the ingredient mixture into 1 to 2 inch balls, place them in a large frying pan and cook until browned. Remove from heat and set aside.
Heat your favorite Spagehetti sauce in a separate saucepan. Bring to boil. Reduce heat. Slowly stir in meatballs and simmer until heated throughout. Serve over noodles.

name: Meatloaf
directions: Mix all ingredients, bake on baking sheet for about 1 hour, enjoy.

name: Laura's Meatloaf
directions: Combine eggs, oatmeal, milk, worcestershire sauce, salt, garlic powder, pepper, mustard, onion.  Mix with hamburger.  Pat into loaf pan.  Bake @350 for one hour.  Combine ketchup, brown sugar and mustard.  Spread over meatloaf.  Bake 20-25 minutes longer.

name: Inside out Bacon Cheeseburgers
directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
In bowl combine hamburg and 1 cup dressing.
Make 12 patties.  Place 6 on pan.
In bowl combine bacon and remaining dressing.
Place a spoonful of bacon mixture in center of each patty on the pan.
Place a slice of cheese on top.
Top with remaining patties.
Pinch edges tightly.
Bake 15-20 minutes, flip and bake another 15-20 minutes or until hamburger is done.

similar.name: Lea & Perrins Bacon and Cheese Burgers
All Mixed up Bacon Cheese Burgers

name: Savory Meatballs
directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Combine everything in bowl and mix well.
Make into 1-2" balls and place on pan.
Bake 20 - 25 minutes or until done.
Serve with spaghetti and sauce.

name: Easy Baked Meatballs
directions: preheat oven to 400 degrees.
in large bowl combine all ingrediants.
mix all ingrediants.
roll about 15 meatballs.
place on baking dish.
bake uncovered for 20-25 minutes.

name: Blue Cheese & Bacon Burgers
directions: Mix together the softened butter and blue cheese until well mixed.    Roll into a log about 2-3 inches in diameter and wrap in Parchment paper.    Refrigerate until hardened.   You can also freeze if you don't plan to use for a while.
Once the butter is hardened, mix together the remaining ingredients in a large bowl by hand.   Form the meat into 4-6 balls (depending on how big you want your burgers).

Slice 1/2 inch slices from hardened blue cheese/butter log.    Place each butter/blue cheese circle into the middle of the burger ball and press into middle, shaping into a thick burger shape.  Repeat for all remaining burgers. Ensure that butter/blue cheese is sealed into the middle of the meat.     Grill burgers until desired doneness.
Cook bacon as usual on stove or oven.   If you have non-peppered bacon, add a generous amount of pepper before cooking.
Serve burgers on your favorite bun with bacon.   Add your favorite toppings -- fresh tomatoes, lettuce, extra blue cheese, ketchup, mustard, extra blue cheese, etc.
These are awesome!   I love to make them huge -- 1/3 to 1/2 pounders and serve without a bun and have a salad as a side dish.   They are so yummy!

similar.name: All Mixed up Bacon Cheese Burgers

name: Italian Meatballs
directions: With your ground chuck, you'll want to add in the ingredients at once and blend with your hands.  Eyeball the breadcrumbs and add more along the way as you see fit to form a good solid meatball.

name: Cocktail Meatballs
directions: Mix all ingredients for meatballs.
Shape into bite-sized balls.
Bake at 350 degrees for approximately 20 minutes.
Combine sauce ingredients and mix well.
Add cooked meatballs and simmer for approximately 30 minutes.

name: Cheeseburger Meatloaf
directions: Preheat oven to 375°F Mix meat, cracker crumbs and mustard until well blended.
Form meat mixture into 8-inch loaf pan. Bake 40 minute or until cooked through.
Spread meat loaf with ketchup; sprinkle with cheese. Bake 5 minute or until cheese is melted.

name: Mozzarella Stuffed Meatballs
directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
In a medium bowl, soak the breadcrumbs in the milk.
Add the beef, garlic, parsley, Parmesan cheese, egg and 1 tsp salt to the soaked breadcrumbs and stir with a fork until combined.
Place about 2 tablespoons of mixture in your hand and press a mozzarella cube into the center. Shape the meat around the cheese, forming a ball. Repeat with remaining mozzarella.
Cook at 350 degrees for 25-30 minutes.

name: Easy Baked Parmesan Meatballs
directions: Preheat oven to 375°F
Mix meat, cheese, parsley, egg and garlic.
Shape into 12 meatballs.
Place in foil-lined 15x10x1- inch baking pan.
Bake 25 minutes or until cooked through.

name: Italian Meat Loaf
directions: Mix all the ingredients together except parsley. Mix very well and form into a oval loaf pan or glass baking dish, about 9 inches x 5 inches. Bake at 350 for 1 hour.
Remove from the oven and let stand 10 minutes. Garnish with chopped parsley.

name: Baked Meatballs
directions: Pour olive oil on a paper towel and rub over a baking sheet with raised sides.
Toss milk and bread in a LARGE bowl.
Add the pressed garlic, grated onion and stir well.
Add the cheese, parsley, red pepper flakes, allspice, salt and pepper.
Beat the eggs, add to the bread mixture and stir until all ingredients are well incorporated.
Add the ground beef and mix very gently with your finger tips, just until all ingredients are blended (over handling the meat will make the meatballs tough), just toss, turn and fluff it.
Gently roll the meat mixture into 2 inch balls.
Place the meatballs on the prepared roasting pan, making sure they are well spaced and refrigerate for one hour to let the flavors meld.
Pre-heat the oven to 400.
Bake the meatballs for 17 minutes.

To freeze the cooked meatballs:  Place them on a baking sheet, making sure they are not touching each other and place in the freezer for two hours.  Place frozen meatballs in a freezer safe bag and back into the freezer.
Enjoy!

name: Mmm Meatloaf
directions: mix all ingredients together and bake at 350 d. f. for 45-60 minute.

name: Swedish Style Meatballs
directions: Melt 2 tbsp butter in large skillet over medium heat.  Add onions.  Cook and stir 8 to 10 minutes or until onions are ver soft.  Remove from heat and set aside.
Combine veal, beef, pork, bread crumbs, beaten eggs, 1/2 tsp salt, pepper and nutmeg in large bowl.  Add onions, mix well.  Using 2 tbsp meat mixture per meatball, shape into 36 balls.  Set aside.

Preheat oven to 200 degrees.  Reheat skillet over medium heat.  Add 1/4 to 1/2 of meatballs.  Do not crowd skillet.  Cook 8 minutes, shaking skillet to allow meatballs to roll and brown evenly.  Reduce heat to medium low.  Cook 15 minutes or until cooked through.  Transfer to covered casserole dish and keep warm in oven.  Repeat until all meatballs are cooked.

Wipe out skillet.  Melt remaining 2 tbsp butter over medium heat.  Whisk in flour, stir well.  Combine milk, half and half, egg yolk and remaining 1/2 tsp salt in small bowl.  Slowly mix flour mixture.  Reduce heat to medium low.  Cook and stir 3 minutes or until thickened.  Remove dish from oven and pour sauce over meatballs.
Serve immediately.

name: Ketchup Meatloaf
directions: Mix in a large bowl, put in a 4" X 8" glass dish.Then put ketchup on top of the loaf.

name: All Mixed up Bacon Cheese Burgers
directions: Thoroughly combine all ingredients in a medium bowl.
Shape into 4 patties.
Grill or broil until done.

similar.name: Lea & Perrins Bacon and Cheese Burgers

name: Spaghetti Meatballs
directions: in a bowl, combine beef and sauce mix.
shape into 1 inch balls.
place in a shallow 2 quart microwave-safe dish.
cover and microwave on high for 3 minutes.
rotate a quarter turn.
microwave 2 minutes longer, or until meat is no longer pink.
drain.
use in your favorite spaghetti sauce recipe.

name: Garlic Cheddar Cheese Hamburgers
directions: Mix beef in bowl with salt, pepper and paprika.
Divide in 4 patties.
Form patties around cubes (2 or more) of cheese.
Make sure all cheese is covered by meat and patties are not too thin.
Heat pan on high heat.
Cook burger for 6-7 minutes on one side.
Flip and cook for another 6 min turning the heat to medium.
Serve on a toasted bun with sliced bacon, tomato and your favorite sauce!

name: Mike's Italian Meatballs
directions: Combine all ingredients into mixing bowl.  Using a tablespoon, spoon out a meatball, shape and place in greased baking pan.  Once all meatballs are placed, cook in a 350 degree oven for 20-25 minutes until done.  Then I drop into a pot of homemade spaghetti sauce and serve over farfalle pasta.

name: Jim Harrison's Meatballs
directions: SAUCE:

Pour a liberal amount of good olive oil in the bottom of the baking pan. Halve a dozen or so tomatoes and place them in the pan. Sprinkle them liberally with chopped garlic, fresh basil, and thyme. Cook for about an hour at 325°F Chop this roughly and you have your sauce.
MEATBALLS:

Saute the garlic, finely chopped, in olive oil until translucent. Add the anchovies and let melt, then add the parsely and wilt; let cool. Mix the meat with the eggs. Add the cooked garlic, anchovies, parsley, salt and pepper, and bread crumbs. Form your meatballs, but not too large or they will crack. Brown in olive oil, then cook slowly. Mix in your roasted tomato sauce.".

name: The Best Meatballs Ever
directions: Mix all ingredients well.
Shape into meatballs.(I make the meatballs a good handful. They are probably 1 1/2 inches in diameter or more).
You may freeze for later use or refrigerate for 24 hours.
Bake at 350 for 20 to 25 minutes. Test for doneness because it does depend on the size of your meatball.
These may also be simmered in your sauce without baking. I bake them on a roasting rack to let the fat drip into the pan below, but you don't have to.

To cook in sauce,drop gently into simmering sauce and don't stir for at least 25 minutes or the meatballs will break. Simmer for a total of about 45 for your meatballs to be done. Test one to be sure.
Skim the fat off the top of the sauce if you simmer the meatballs.

name: Moist and Flavorful Meatloaf
directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Saute onion, bell pepper and garlic in a little oil over medium heat until translucent, about 3-5 minutes. Set aside to cool.
Put all ingredients except milk in a large bowl.
Stir to combine with one hand while you add milk with the other* until all ingredients are uniformly mixed. Do not squish the ingredients together, this may make the meatloaf tough and dense.
Form into a loaf placed in baking dish. Stir together topping ingredients and brush on meatloaf.
Bake 1 hours and 15 minute at 350 degrees.
Let rest 3-5 minutes before slicing.
*not sure if this is totally necessary, it's just what I did and I liked my texture, if it is too difficult just add all ingredients at once.

name: Amazing Burgers
directions: Put all your ingredients- aside from ground beef- in a big bowl and stir everything together. Melt the butter before adding it.

Break the ground beef up into little pieces and add to the bowl and mix everything together thoroughly. I make the mix into as dense a log as possible and then cut it into 6 pieces to ensure uniform size in the patties.
Form your patties.
Spray each patty lightly with cooking spray and then season with salt and pepper. Use your hands to gently pat the salt and pepper into each patty.
Fire up the grill and grab a cold one.

name: Freezer Meatballs
directions: Preheat oven to 400*. In large bowl, combine eggs and milk; blend well. Stir in remaining ingredients; mix well. Shape into 36 meatballs; place in ungreased 15 x 10" pan. Bake 15 to 20 minutes or until lightly browned and thoroughly cooked. Cool 5 minutes; place in 13 x 9" pan. Freeze, uncovered 45 minutes; place 12 partially frozen meatballs in each of 3 containers or bags and return to freezer for later use.

name: Meatloaf
directions: Position a rack in the center of an oven and preheat to 350º F.

In a large fry pan over medium-high heat, warm the oil. When the oil is hot, add the onion, celery and garlic and sauté, stirring occasionally, until tender and translucent, 6 to 8 minutes. Set aside.

Tear the bread slices into small pieces, transfer to a food processor and pulverize the bread into crumbs. In a large mixing bowl, combine the bread crumbs and milk, stir until blended and let stand for 2 to 3 minutes.

Add the onion-celery mixture to the bread mixture and stir until blended. Add the beef, pork, veal, tomato sauce, egg, salt and pepper and fold together gently until blended. Transfer the mixture to a 2 1/2-lb. loaf pan and, using your hands, shape the mixture into an oblong loaf. Spread the ketchup evenly over the top. Bake until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the center of the meat loaf registers 165ºF, about 1 1/2 hours.

name: Cheeseburger
directions: fry beef in the pan.  Put it on a bun.

name: Meatballs
directions: in a large bowl, place meat and rub garlic into it.  Press meat down into sides of bowl and add: salt, pepper, parsley, 2 eggs, water.  sprinkle parmesan cheese and then breadcrumbs.  Fold mixture together.
Bake on lightly greased sheet at 350 for 30+ minutes and then put in sauce to continue to cook.  The longer they cook in sauce, the better the sauce.

name: Meatloaf Stuffed With Cheese
directions: grind garlic and onion very fine in a chopper or food processor especialy if your family  does not like onions  then mix all in a large bowl except the cheese  mix good

then take a cookie sheet an  spread out to fill the  cookie sheet  at the top place the cheese i use one cup motzarella an one cup sharp cheddar  but  you can  use  any  then i pull up  the sides  then here is  my trick  the meatloaf is  long an thin  i press the sides up an take my hands an make a line down the middle  this allows the loaf  to  breath  an the cheese not  to  ooze out the  sides
leave on cookie  sheet  bake at  350 for at least  45 minutes.

name: Grilled Steak Burgers
directions: Combine meat, egg and Worcestershire sauce.  (I use around 2 Tbsp or a little more).
Mix with your hands.
Divide mixture and form into patties.  Place on foil-lined cookie sheet.

Add salt, pepper, garlic salt.  If you are grilling, double the amounts, because as it grills, the seasoning will also drain off.  If you are cooking in a pan, be much more conservative because it will not drain off.
Cook to desired doneness.
Serve with your favorite hamburger toppings.

name: Tex-Mex Burgers
directions: Mix ground chuck with egg, worcestershire, salt, and pepper.
Form into 4 or 5 patties about 1/2 inch thick, and 4 inches in diameter.
Cook on grill for 5-6 minutes on each side, or in a skillet for 4-5 minutes.
Spread some guacamole, and jalapeno peppers on each bun.

name: Perfect Hamburger
directions: In an airtight container or plastic bag, mix together salt, black pepper, onion powder, garlic powder, ground mustard, smoked paprika, and thyme.
Shake until well mixed.
In a bowl, combine ground beef, Worcestershire sauce and approximately 2 tablespoons of the seasoning mixture.
Make into patties and grill or pan fry.
Enjoy with your favorite toppings!

name: Chris' Burgers
directions: Preheat an outdoor grill for high heat. Mix together the ground beef, garlic, olive oil, salt, pepper, and basil. Divide into four balls, and flatten into patties.  Make a thumb imprint in the middle of each patty so they cook level.

Cook the patties for about 3 to 5 minutes on each side, or to desired doneness. The internal temperature should be at least 160 degrees F (70 degrees C). Remove from grill and place onto hamburger buns. Top with desired toppings and condiments.

name: Grilled Chile Pepper Cheeseburgers
directions: In a medium bowl, combine green onion, yogurt, jalapenos, pepper and salt.  Add beef and mix well.
Shape beef mixture into 8 patties about 3/4 inch thick.  Place wax paper between the patties and freeze in a 1-gallon freezer bag.
To prepare, thaw patties in the refrigerator.  Grill until no pink remains in the center.  Turn once.  Top each patty with cheese the last 2 minutes of grilling time.

name: Grandpas Meatballs
directions: Put ground beef and pork in a bowl.
Mix egg and milk and add to beef and pork.
Add the rest of the ingredients.
Mix well.
Roll into golf ball size balls.
Add to simmering pasta sauce to cook at least an hour.

name: Big Dog Hamburgers
directions: Combine eggs and milk in a mixing bowl.
Stir in bread crumbs, onion, mustard and garlic.
Mix in ground beef.
Form 1 inch thick patties.
Season with salt and pepper.
Grill until they are cooked all the way through.
Serve on a toasted bun with choice of condiments.

name: Quick & Easy Grilled Hamburgers
directions: Combine ingredients, taking care not to over mix. Add a tablespoon of water if need be. Make into 6 - 8 patties. Grill on outside grill. We use a gas grill at 300 degrees and cook time is about 10 minutes, flipping the burgers a couple of times. Eliminate excessive grease by pressing the burgers after each flip. Add cheese of your choice or none at all -- serve hot on hamburger buns or eat just plain.

name: Cindy's Meatballs
directions: In a large bowl, combine above ingredients. Shape into 20 (1 1/2 inch) meatballs. Place meatballs on a baking sheet. Bake in oven for 15 to 20 minutes in a preheated 350 degree oven. eanwhile, in a large skillet or pot, heat pasta sauce. Add meatballs and simmer until heated through.

name: David's Hamburgers
directions: Mix all ingredients and form into patties.
Cook to desired doneness.

name: Easy Grilled Hamburgers
directions: Mix all ingredients together well and place covered mixture in the refrigerator for several hours to marinate before grilling.
From the mound of burger, divide into four sections.
Make patties and add to hot grill.
Try to flip only once or twice, and do not press down on the burger with the spatula. ( you will lose moisture).
Just before burgers are done, toast the rolls.

name: Meatloaf Like Boston Market
directions: Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
Boil tomato paste, BBQ sauce, and sugar in a saucepan.  Once it boils remove from heat.
In a large bowl, add the meat, egg and 1/4 cup of the sauce.
Mix well with your hands.
Add the breadcrumbs, salt, onion powder, pepper, garlic powder to the meat and use your hands to mix well.
Place the meat mixture into a loaf pan.  (I like the 2 piece kind that drains the fat.).
Place the remaining sauce on top of the meatloaf.
Bake meatloaf for one hour and let rest before slicing.

name: Steak N' Bacon Cheddar Meatballs
directions: Preheat oven to 450.  Brush 13x9 pan with 1 tablespoons Olive Oil.
1)  Heat 1 tablespoons Olive Oil in large skillet at medium heat.  Add onions and bacon, saute, stir often until golden brown, approximately 10 minutes.
2)  Remove, drain and cool.
3)  Mix bacon, onions and the rest of the ingredients.
4)  Roll into golf ball size, tightly.
5)  Roast for 18-20 minutes.  Cool for 5 minutes.  Enjoy!

name: Baked Meatballs
directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Place the water, onion, garlic, and parsley in a blender or food processor and puree.
In a large bowl, combine the pureed mix, meat, bread crumbs, egg, Parmesan or Romano cheese, red pepper flakes, and salt.
Combine with hands until mixture is uniform, do not over mix.
Form mixture into balls 1-1/2" to 2" diameter.  I use foil muffing tins to cook in (easy clean-up).
Bake for 20-30 minutes, depending on size and oven.

name: Basic Meatloaf (Southern Style)
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.

Mix ground beef, salt, pepper, eggs, bread crumbs, milk, Worcestershire, onion, thyme, and rosemary. (I whisk dried ingredients and pour over hamburger and fold together lightly, first. Then I add wet ingredients and incorporate together really well. I usually add less milk or add extra bread crumbs to preferred consistency).
Pack into a lightly greased loaf pan or form into a loaf and place on a lightly greased baking sheet with sides, such as a jelly roll pan. (I form into a loaf in a 9 X 13 pyrex baking dish).
Top meatloaf with sliced bacon, or your choice of ketchup or barbecue sauce.
Bake at 350° for 1 1/2 - 1 3/4 hours, or till 170 to 180 degrees.
Dianna suggests serving meatloaf with heated tomato sauce or gravy.  (I suggest serving with garlic mashed potatoes and mixed vegetables for a comfort style meal).

name: Chop-Your-Own Burgers
directions: In a food processor, pulse the Parmesan until very finely ground; it should resemble breadcrumbs. Transfer to a large bowl and set aside.

Cut the steak tips into 1-inch chunks, then arrange them in a single layer on a rimmed baking sheet. Place the baking sheet in the freezer for 15 to 20 minutes, or until the pieces of beef are firm, but not frozen solid.

Once the beef is firm, place half of the pieces in the processor (there is no need to wash it out ofter the Parmesan), then pulse until well chopped, but not ground. This should take about 1 minute of on-off pulsing. Transfer the chopped beef to the bowl with the cheese, then repeat the procedure with the remaining beef.
Drizzle the fish sauce and soy sauce over the copped beef, then use your hands to mix everything together. Make sure the cheese is evenly distributed, but try not to knead or overwork the meat.

Divide the meat into 4 portions, then form each into a burger patty. As you form the patties, use your thumb to press an indentation into the center of each burger. This prevents the burgers from bulging in the center during cooking. The burgers can be grilled immediately or refrigerated for up to 2 days.
Grill the burgers over high heat for 1 to 2 minutes per side or until well seared, then either move the burgers to a cooler part of the grill or reduce the flame and cook to desired doneness.

name: Red Pepper Cheddar Meatloaf
directions: Preheat oven to 400 degrees.
Combine all ingredients EXCEPT ground beef. When well combined, gently mix in ground beef.
Form loaf and bake for approx 35-40 minutes uncovered.

name: Homemade Glazed Meatloaf
directions: Preheat oven to 350°F  Combined Knorr Recipe Onion Soup Mix, ground beef, bread crumbs, eggs and 1/2 cup ketchup in large bowl.
Shape into loaf in meat loaf baking pan.  Bake uncovered 40 minute  Meanwhile, combine remaining 1/2 cup ketchup and vinegar in medium bowl.
Spread ketchup mixture evenly over meatloaf and continue baking 20 min, or until done. Let stand 10 min before serving.

name: The Best Ever Burgers
directions: In a small bowl, combine all ingredients except hamburger and bread crumbs, mixing well.
In a large bowl, add hamburger and break it up a bit.
Pour wet ingredients over top of hamburger and mix just enough to combine.
Sprinkle bread crumbs over mix and combine.  Do not over mix as it will make the meat tougher.
Form 1/4 pound patties (or whatever size you prefer).  You should get about nine 1/4 pound patties out of the mix.
(You can cook immediately or let rest, covered, in refrigerator for a few hours.).
Cook on grill or  pan fry until done as desired.
